blocks.text: The righteous man perishes, And no one considers; Pious men are taken away, And no one gives thought That because of evil The righteous was taken away.
Yet he shall come to peace, He shall have rest on his couch Who walked straightforward.
Heb. “They shall have rest on their couches.”
But as for you, come closer, You sons of a sorceress, You offspring of an adulterer and a harlot!
Lit. “she acts the harlot.”
With whom do you act so familiarly? At whom do you open your mouth And stick out your tongue? Why, you are children of iniquity, Offspring of treachery—
You who inflame yourselves Among the terebinths, Under every verdant tree; Who slaughter children in the wadis, Among the clefts of the rocks.
I.e., in some frenzied idolatrous rite.
Heb. “under.”
With such are your share and portion, They, they are your allotment; To them you have poured out libations, Presented offerings. Should I relent in the face of this?
Meaning of Heb. uncertain.
The cult-trees referred to above in v. 5.
On a high and lofty hill You have set your couch; There, too, you have gone up To perform sacrifices.

Behind the door and doorpost You have directed your thoughts; Abandoning Me, you have gone up On the couch you made so wide. You have made a covenant with them, You have loved bedding with them; You have chosen lust.
Meaning of Heb. uncertain.
The cult-trees referred to above in v. 5.
I.e., with the objects behind door and doorpost.
Like Ugaritic yd, from root ydd, “to love.”
You have approached the king with oil, You have provided many perfumes. And you have sent your envoys afar, Even down to the netherworld.
Or “Molech.”
I.e., you have brought tribute to alien cults as to a king.
Though wearied by much travel, You never said, “I give up!” You found gratification for your lust, And so you never cared.
Whom do you dread and fear, That you tell lies? But you gave no thought to Me, You paid no heed. It is because I have stood idly by so long That you have no fear of Me.
Emendation yields “Them you dreaded and feared, / And so you gave them thought.”
Emendation yields “and shut My eyes.”
I hereby pronounce judgment upon your deeds: Your assorted [idols] shall not avail you,
Lit. “your retribution and your deeds.”
Brought up from v. 13 for clarity.
Shall not save you when you cry out. They shall all be borne off by the wind, Snatched away by a breeze. But those who trust in Me shall inherit the land And possess My sacred mount.
[The Lord] says: Build up, build up a highway! Clear a road! Remove all obstacles From the road of My people!

For thus said He who high aloft Forever dwells, whose name is holy: I dwell on high, in holiness; Yet with the contrite and the lowly in spirit— Reviving the spirits of the lowly, Reviving the hearts of the contrite.
For I will not always contend, I will not be angry forever: Nay, I who make spirits flag, Also create the breath of life.
Meaning of Heb. uncertain.
For their sinful greed I was angry; I struck them and turned away in My wrath. Though stubborn, they follow the way of their hearts,
I.e., Israel’s. Cf. “My people,” v. 14.
Meaning of Heb. uncertain. Emendation yields “When they have walked broken in the contrition of their hearts.”
I note how they fare and will heal them: I will guide them and mete out solace to them, And to the mourners among them
heartening, comforting words: It shall be well, Well with the far and the near —said the Lord— And I will heal them.
Lit. “the vigor of”; cf. Eccl. 12.1 and postbiblical bori.
The Heb. nib is otherwise unknown; its meaning is inferred from that of nid (cf. the verb nad “to condole”) in the parallel expression in Job 16.5.
But the wicked are like the troubled sea Which cannot rest, Whose waters toss up mire and mud.
There is no safety —said my God— For the wicked.
